body.title: Pre-inference context assembler script
body.result: script at lab/claudecode/pre_inference_assemble.py: pattern matching, symbol map, domain terms; 12 tests
body.intention: I intend that a script assembles a complete pre-inference context block from a ticket's affected files, pattern inventory, and word graph traversal — before any LLM call fires.
body.description:
A script (tools/pre_inference_assemble.py or similar) that takes a ticket ID and produces a pre-assembled context block. Steps: load patterns inventory doc, extract file symbols via existing repo_map.py, run word graph traversal for domain terms from ticket title/description, format as structured context block ready to prepend to the LLM prompt. No LLM involved — pure file reads, AST, and graph traversal.

**Affected files:** TBD — new script; calls existing tools/repo_map.py and word graph machinery; reads docs/design_patterns_inventory.md

**Design rules:** none apply — new tooling file

**Scope boundary:** In scope: file symbol extraction, pattern inventory load, word graph lookup, structured output. Out of scope: small-model arch summarization (future ticket), LLM involvement, modifying sprint-ticket (that's T-sprint-wire-pre-inference).

**Completion criteria:** Script accepts a ticket ID, reads affected files from ticket metadata, and emits a context block to stdout containing: matched patterns, file symbol map, word graph hits for ticket domain terms. Runnable standalone: python tools/pre_inference_assemble.py T-xxx produces non-empty output for any ticket with known affected files.
